member.name: Neil Duncan-Jordan
member.synopsis: Neil Duncan-Jordan is the Labour MP for Poole , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Trade Union Officials
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether he plans to give elected workplace Green reps rights to information, consultation and participation in workplace sustainability and transition planning, including through joint Futureproofing C
writtenQuestions.answerText: While there is no separate statutory framework for green representatives, the Employment Rights Act 2025 strengthened rights to reasonable paid time off for trade union representatives and trade union equality representatives. Where a workplace green repr...

writtenQuestions.heading: Trade Agreements: Faroe Islands
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether his Department plans to include (a) animal welfare and (b) environmental conservation on the agenda of the next meeting of the Joint Committee established under the UK-Faroe Islands Free Trade
writtenQuestions.answerText: Officials meet annually for the UK-Faroe Islands FTA Joint Committee, with the latest held on 3rd June 2026 in London. The FTA establishes effective channels for UK officials from the Department for Business and Trade to lobby the Faroes on animal welfare...

writtenQuestions.heading: Development Aid: Expenditure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether she has carried out an impact assessment on the decision to further reduce the Official Development Aid budget.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There has been no further reduction in the budget for Official Development Assistance (ODA) since the announcement by HM Treasury in Spending Review 2025 of department spending allocations up to 2028/29, published on 11 June 2025. A full Equality Impact A...

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps are being taken to address delays in the Armed Forces pension remedy programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence (MOD) is committed to issuing all Armed Forces Pension Scheme (AFPS) members with their entitlement as quickly and efficiently as possible, and the delivery of Remedial Service Statements (RSS) continues to be prioritised. While si...

writtenQuestions.heading: Oil: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to ensure that changes in oil prices are reflected at UK petrol stations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that motorists get a fair deal at the pump and we have taken several measures to protect consumers from fuel prices increases. We have extended the temporary 5p fuel duty cut until 31 December 2026 which will save t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: WhatsApp: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether she will make an assessment of the potential merits of the inclusion of WhatsApp in any social media ban for under 16s.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government does not intend for messaging services to be included in social media companies being banned from offering services to under 16s. This is to support children staying in touch with family, friends and communities. Final details of restrictio...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Free School Meals: Adoption
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether the Government has assessed the combined impact of reducing adoption support funding while also changing school meal eligibility.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The adoption and special guardianship support fund (ASGSF) provides important therapeutic support for adopted and special guardianship order children. The changes made in 2025/26 financial year were to ensure support for all those applying for ASGSF. The ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Arms Trade: Military Bases
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to help ensure that arms transferred through UK military bases are used in accordance with international law.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK continues to be committed to upholding International Law and will always operate in accordance with its key principals.The use of UK military bases by foreign governments is governed by a range of longstanding agreements. For operational security r...

writtenQuestions.heading: Gaza: Surveillance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether he will release Gaza surveillance flights footage of 25 and 26 March 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Following the terrorist attacks against Israel of 7 October 2023, the UK government worked with partners across the region to secure the release of hostages, including British nationals, who were kidnapped. In support of these efforts, the Ministry of Def...

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Admissions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of faith-based oversubscription criteria in school admissions on (a) racial and (b) ethnic groups.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Faith schools are an important part of our diverse school system and parliament has legislated so that they can make certain choices about their admission arrangements. The Equality Act 2010 permits designated faith schools and academies to use faith-base...

writtenQuestions.heading: Immigration: EU Nationals
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, if her Department will publish statistics on (a) eVisa errors, (b) cancellations of EUSS status and (c) EUSS status granted in error.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The statistics on eVisa errors are available here:https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/online-immigration-status-evisa-ukvi-account-creation-data/evisa-error-correction-volumes-from-the-online-webformThere are currently no plans to publish the data ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Disability 20
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether the Government plans to set up an engagement group of disabled people's organisations to be involved in decision making associated with the work of the D20.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Decisions on the membership and UK representatives of G20 engagement groups are not for the Government to make; they should be decided by the groups themselves.

writtenQuestions.heading: Disability 20
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether the UK's lead in the D20 will be a person with a disability.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Decisions on the membership and UK representatives of G20 engagement groups are not for the Government to make; they should be decided by the groups themselves.

writtenQuestions.heading: Disability 20
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, who will be the UK's lead from 15 December 2026 when the UK takes over the chair of the D20.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Decisions on the membership and UK representatives of G20 engagement groups are not for the Government to make; they should be decided by the groups themselves.
